prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Use the current frame-business-research-problem Skill at skills/frame-business-research-problem/. Follow its readiness, fidelity, managerial-framing, scholarly-positioning, evidence, boundary, and mode instructions. Use only the serialized case material.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": true,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "jobs",
    "eligibility",
    "capacities",
    "failure-scenario distribution",
    "costs"
  ],
  "domain": "OR",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "Failure scenarios represent next-day availability",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "Machine availability is exogenous rather than chosen",
    "First-stage assignments precede availability and recourse remains limited"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "state_as_decision",
    "future_or_hidden_information",
    "model_changed_for_story"
  ],
  "id": "or-12",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "machine capacity",
      "job eligibility",
      "scenario-specific reassignment limit"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "first-stage job assignments",
      "second-stage feasible reassignments"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "minimize advance assignment, recourse, and uncompleted-job cost",
    "mechanism": "Advance assignments position work before failures, while limited reassignment responds after exogenous machine availability is observed.",
    "model_type": "two-stage stochastic machine-assignment model",
    "state_variables": [
      "realized machine availability",
      "uncompleted jobs"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "next-day machine availability scenarios"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "The planner chooses which machines remain available",
    "Advance assignments use realized failure information"
  ],
  "prompt": "Before next-day operations, a planner assigns jobs using a distribution of machine failures. Actual machine availability is then realized exogenously, after which limited reassignment is allowed. A proposed narrative says the planner chooses which machines will remain available. Diagnose the state-as-decision error and restore both decision stages.",
  "tags": [
    "stochastic-programming",
    "machine-availability",
    "state-variable",
    "adversarial"
  ],
  "title": "Stochastic machine availability miscast as a planner choice"
}
```
